Subject: Re: [opensuse] Intel 3945 Wireless Problem opensuse 10.3 - RESOLVED!
Body: Joe Sloan wrote: > Chuck Stuettgen wrote: > >> The fix is to in YAST, select Configure on the Wireless Card. Select the >> Hardware Tab, then change the Kernel Module in the Module Name Drop down >> list from the ipw3945 to the iwl3945. Save the settings and exit out of >> YAST. Reboot the laptop. >> >> If the iwl3945 kernel module is not in the list install it first then >> reboot and change. > > > Ack, that is a good fix - except for the reboots; absolutely no need for > any of that, just rmmod the ipw3945 and you're good to go. Here's something I just found and points to where the "real" problem with hidden sites is, when for awhile I assumed it was KNetworkmanager....so did others. For almost 2 weeks, I've been using the ipw3945 driver and the standard ifup method to connect. I just changed from that driver to the iwl3945 driver, stopped the ipw driver and used Yast to setup using the iwl driver with KNetworkmanager.....just to see what happend. I've NOT been able to see hidden sites, including my own at home, with KNetworkmanager.......now I can. So, it would appear that this problem that plagues some others with different chipsets have a problem with the driver, NOT KNetworkmanager. Thanks to Chuck Stuettgen for pointing this out!! Fred -- A: Because it messes up the order in which people normally read text. Q: Why is top-posting such a bad thing?

Subject: TikTok v. Montana
Body: Forbes NewslettersPlus: U.S. Workers Are Using Weed At Record Levels, But Are They High On The Job? ADVERTISEMENTBECOME A MEMBER βÄΔ Newsletters βÄΔ MyForbesGood morning, As someone who drives a dated (arguably ancient) car, I know all too well the frustration that comes with recall noticesβÄîCarfax says there have been around 200 βÄ€Do Not DriveβÄù or βÄ€Park OutsideβÄù vehicle recalls in the past two decades. Still, it was shocking to read that an estimated 2.5 million models with a serious recall are still unrepaired. California, Texas and Florida lead the nation with over 200,000 recalled vehicles each. Carfax maintains a complete list of recalled vehicles and affected model years here, and prospective buyers can also check used cars that could be at risk here.Drive safe out there, Sarah Whitmire Senior Editor, Newsletters Follow me on Twitter and Forbes.comBREAKING NEWSAP/Alex Brandon President Joe Biden and House Speaker Kevin McCarthy (R-Calif.) both said their meeting last night to discuss raising the debt ceiling was βÄ€productive,βÄù though itβÄôs still not clear how the two leaders will find common ground. Meanwhile, Treasury Secretary Janet Yellen reiterated in a letter to Speaker McCarthy Monday that it is βÄ€highly likelyβÄù the government could run out of money to pay off its bills in 10 days.E. Jean Carroll asked a federal court to award new damages after former President Donald Trump denied sexually assaulting her at a CNN town hall earlier this month. βÄ€It makes a mockery of the jury verdict and our justice system if he can just keep on repeating the same defamatory statements over and over again,βÄù CarrollβÄôs attorney said. BUSINESS + FINANCEAP/Lawrence Neumeister, FileCharlie Javice, founder of defunct student loan assistance company Frank, pleaded not guilty to fraud charges for allegedly lying about her companyβÄôs database when she sold it to JPMorgan Chase in 2021 for $175 million. Javice was arrested last month on charges from the DOJ and SECβÄîand is also facing a separate lawsuit from JPMorgan. Analysts at Bank of America have high hopes for the S&P 500 this year, raising its year-end price target for the index by 8% on the optimism of companiesβÄô recent cost-cutting and the AI boom in tech stocks. ThatβÄôd be the S&PβÄôs highest level since August, but only 2% higher than where the index landed yesterday. WEALTH + ENTREPRENEURSHIPThe dollar is weak, the world is hungry and the Russians are making a mess of the grain markets. Farmer-Financier Sal GilbertieβÄôs food-based inflation wedge could add some spice to your portfolio, when conditions are right. His Teucrium Trading offers pure-play exchange-traded funds that hold futures positions in corn, wheat, soybeans and sugar. TECH + INNOVATIONTo the surprise of likely no one, TikTok sued the state of Montana for its ban signed into law last week that would take effect next year. The suit alleges MontanaβÄôs new law suppresses free speech allowed under the First Amendment and seeks to have the ban overturned, permanently prevented from implementation and declared unconstitutional.Cryptocurrency platform Gemini Exchange planned to file a $1 billion claim against Genesis, putting pressure on Digital Currency Group after it missed a $630 million payment last week. Genesis filed for bankruptcy at the start of 2023 in the wake of the FTX collapse, and DCG owes its crypto lender unit over $1.65 billion. SPORTS + ENTERTAINMENTGetty Images10-time NBA all-star Carmelo Anthony announced he would retire from basketball Monday after 19 seasons in the league. The 38-year-old retires as the No. 9 scorer in league history, and having made more than $260 million in playing salary and bonuses during his career. More: Could LeBron be next? He hinted as much in a postgame news conference last night.TRAVEL + LIFESTYLEThe NAACP issued a formal travel advisory for Florida: βÄ€Under the leadership of Governor DeSantis, the state of Florida has become hostile to Black Americans,βÄù president and CEO Derrick Johnson wrote. It follows a similar travel warning from the League of United Latin American Citizens last week. As recently as March, the state was setting tourism records, and Florida welcomed a record 137.6 million visitors last year. Daily Cover Story Getty ImagesA Record Number Of American Workers Are Using CannabisβÄîThat DoesnβÄôt Mean TheyβÄôre High On The JobRead ArticleTOPLINEThe latest findings from a key drug screening company might lead you to believe U.S. workers need an interventionβÄîa record number of American workers tested positive for marijuana use last year. Overall, Quest Diagnostics found marijuana positivity increased by 10.3% in just one year, from 2021 to 2022.But as any occasional toker whoβÄôs had to pass a drug test knows, that data does not mean workers are actually getting high on the job. People who consume marijuana, and critically, the plantβÄôs psychoactive component THC, can test positive for weeks after exposure. ItβÄôs because THC is lipophilic, meaning it is stored in body fat and can stick around for a while. For this reason, urine tests cannot determine if a person is high at the moment of the test. βÄ€The problem is they don't have a test for impairment,βÄù says Dr. Peter Grinspoon, a primary care physician at Massachusetts General Hospital. βÄ€If you have a positive urine test for cannabisβÄΠit has no implication that you've been impaired before work or even within the last 72 hours.βÄùCurrently, 22 states and Washington, D.C., have legalized recreational use for adults 21 years and older, while 38 states have legal medical marijuana programs. Even though marijuana is still federally illegal, many employers have stopped or minimized screening for weed, including Amazon, and even the FBI and CIA have relaxed their rules around drug testing.WHY IT MATTERSLegalization critics like Kevin Sabet believe the Quest Diagnostics report is a bad omen: βÄ€I think it's worrisome that we're seeing this, especially at our workplaces and in safety sensitive positions, putting other people at riskβÄΠThe issue is whether we think it's okay to be a stoned society or not.βÄù Dr. Grinspoon sees this viewpoint as βÄ€based on moral panic, not the science.βÄù He suggests that βÄ‰zero toleranceβÄô laws, often based on limited drug screening capability, are βÄ€more about persecuting cannabis users than promoting public safety.βÄù MOREWhy Cannabis Jobs Are Going Up In SmokeADVERTISEMENTFACTS AND COMMENTSAs Covid-19 vaccination rates among children continue to lag, a round of recent studies confirmed previous findings that vaccines are effective in children and pregnant people: 32.9%Share of U.S. children ages 5 to 11 who received a Covid vaccine as of early May 5+ millionEstimated number of missed school days in children ages 5 to 17 that couldβÄôve been averted with more widespread adoption of Covid booster vaccines No associationResearchers found no link between Covid vaccines given to pregnant people and spontaneous abortionsSTRATEGY AND SUCCESSItβÄôs a standard way for employers to wrap up a job interview, but it can still stop even the most promising candidates in their tracks: βÄ€Do you have any questions for me?βÄù WeβÄôve got a few suggestions for questions to ask at the end of your next interview, including: Is this a new position? If not, why did the previous employee leave? VIDEOJeffrey Epstein Threatened To Expose Bill GatesβÄô Affair With Russian Bridge Player, Report SaysWatch nowGAMESQUIZThree Southwestern U.S. states struck a deal to reduce their water usage in an attempt to preserve the Colorado River amid historic drought and overuse. Which state was not part of the agreement?A.CaliforniaB.UtahC.NevadaD.ArizonaCheck Your AnswerFRASE BY FORBESUnscramble todayβÄôs anagram to solve this hidden phrase!Scroll to the bottom of todayβÄôs newsletter for a hint.Play Now TODAY'S PHRASE ACROSS THE NEWSROOMAnnouncing the 2023 Forbes 30 Under 30 Local Lists. WeβÄôre looking for the top young founders and leaders in the innovation hubs of Atlanta, Austin, Boston, Chicago, Cleveland, Los Angeles, Miami, Puerto Rico, Seattle and Toronto. The deadline to submit nominations is June 9.BEYOND THE NEWSROOMThe 2023 Forbes Future Of Work Summit will take place in New York and virtually on June 1. WeβÄôll explore the forthcoming impact of artificial intelligence, shifting dynamics between workers and employers, and more. Sign up to stay tuned for updates.Thanks for reading! Follow us on Twitter for by-the-minute updates on the latest business and financial news throughout the day. If youβÄôre playing Frase by Forbes, todayβÄôs hint is "Referral or recommendation passed from one person to another.βÄù Good luck! Subject: Re: Question about Virtual Users
Body: On Sun, Feb 10, 2008 at 06:22:05PM +0100, mouss wrote: > Dominik wrote: > >Hi, > > > >i have the following task to do at my postfix virtual setup: > > > >uiaregi@example.com should be treated as a local user and any other > >E-Mail address at this domain should be threaded as none local. > > > >Does anyone know how to do this? > > virtual_alias_maps: > > uiaregi@example.com gjebvuspsdn@localhost.example.net > > where > - someaccount is a unix user, and > - localhost.example.net is a domain listed in mydestination Simply "someaccount" (without @localhost.blabla or just with @localhost) should be ok, according to the virtual(5) manpage: RESULT ADDRESS REWRITING The lookup result is subject to address rewriting: o When the result has the form @otherdomain, the result becomes the same user in otherdomain. This works only for the first address in a multi-address lookup result. o When "append_at_myorigin=yes", append "@$myorigin" to addresses without "@domain". o When "append_dot_mydomain=yes", (both append_* settings are "yes" by default") Geert

Body:
-----Original Message-----

Subject: please give generously

> >
> >
> >
> > Please give generously...
> >
> > URGENT - DUDLEY EARTHQUAKE APPEAL
> >
> > At 00:54 on Monday 23 September an earthquake measuring 4.8 on the
> >
> > Richter scale hit Dudley,UK causing untold disruption and distress -
> >
> > * Many were woken well before their giro arrived
> >
> > * Several priceless collections of mementos from the Balearics and
> >
> > Spanish costas were damaged
> >
> > * Three areas of historic and scientifically significant litter were
> >
> > disturbed
> >
> > * Thousands are confused and bewildered, trying to come to terms with
> >
> > the fact that something interesting has happened in Dudley
> >
> > One resident, Donna-Marie Dutton, a 17 year old mother-of-three said "It
> >
> > was such a shock, little Chantal-Leanne came running into my bedroom
> >
> > crying. My youngest two, Tyler-Morgan and Megan-Storm slept through it.
> >
> > I was still shaking when I was watching Trisha the next morning."
> >
> > Apparently though, looting did carry on as normal.
> >
> > The British Red Cross have so far managed to ship 4000 crates of Sunny
> >
> > Delight to the area to help the stricken masses.
> >
> > Rescue workers are still searching through the rubble and have found
> >
> > large quantities of personal belongings including benefit books and
> >
> > jewellery from Elizabeth Duke at Argos.
> >
> > HOW YOU CAN HELP
> >
> > * 2 buys chips, scraps and blue pop for a family of four
> >
> > * 10 can take a family to Stourport for the day, where children can
> >
> > play on an unspoiled canal bank among the national collection of
> >
> > stinging nettles
> >
> > * 22p buys a biro for filling in a spurious compensation claim
> >
> > PLEASE ACT NOW
> >
> > Simply email us by return with your credit card details and we'll do the
> >
> > rest! If you prefer to donate cash, there are collection points
> >
> > available at your local branches of Argos, Iceland and Clinton Cards.

Subject: Re: server is rejecting mail from single domain
Body: NOW I get it, I thought that postfix was complaining because host lookup of the FQDN does not match the IP. But to be honest, it looks to me like someone has owned their server and having a bit of fun, because the server is literally announcing itself as "Spam-Server" in the HELO instead of the FQDN! $ telnet 64.22.240.195 25 Trying 64.22.240.195... Connected to 64.22.240.195. Escape character is '^]'. 220 Spam-Server Microsoft ESMTP MAIL Service, Version: 6.0.3790.1830 ready at Fri, 8 Feb 2008 16:55:39 -0800 Sandy Drobic wrote: > Jonathan Dill wrote: >> James D. Parra wrote: >>> I can suggest that to him, but this is a client so I can only ask. >>> From the >>> client's vantage point, our server is the only one rejecting his >>> mail, so I >>> need to tread politely. >>> >>> Concerning removing reject_non_fqdn_hostname (or >>> reject_non_fqdn_helo_hostname) is not something I want to do. If this >>> weren't a client of ours I wouldn't worry too much about it, but it >>> is and I >>> must. Is there a way could add an exception to the main.cf for this >>> domain >>> or IP address? >>> >> >> If he sent e-mail to me, my server would also reject it, then you >> would not be alone. But seriously, I wonder if setting up >> /etc/nsswitch.conf to check "files" first and adding an entry to >> /etc/hosts would fix it e.g. >> >> 64.22.240.195 ns.ky3.com >> >> I am not sure how postfix verifies fqdn, if it uses the OS resolver >> stack or some other method. > > It is not the client hostname that is rejected, it's the helo name > that the client itself determines to announces itself to the receiving > server. > > This is especially stupid since you have to configure the helo of your > own system. > > So you can only kick the admin of the server with the non-fqdn-helo or > whitelist the client. >
